page: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a dedicated frequency of 3.0 GHz. The configuration features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading with exactly 1.0 thread per core. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64pls_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top-25% result in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing. It also exhibits high memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, placing in the top 25% with 4383.1 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write operations perform in the average range. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing workloads on Geekbench both register strong top-25% scores. Conversely, single-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the average tier, and text processing on Geekbench is relatively weak. Redis SET operations are highly efficient, achieving a strong top-25% result of 80.12 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64pls_v5 offers a high physical core density on an ARM architecture, which typically translates to lower power consumption and a favorable performance-per-resource profile for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. However, the low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This server is well-suited for compute-intensive, highly parallelized workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as software compilation, containerized microservices, web serving, and Redis-based caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPLSv5 family, powered by a 64-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, equivalent to 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance and high cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong results in software compilation and Redis operations. However, single-core performance and text processing remain average to weak. Given its low memory-to-core ratio and high parallel processing capability, this server is optimized for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and caching, rather than memory-intensive databases.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 64 dedicated Ampere cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered compute platform featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and a low memory-to-core ratio optimized for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and no local storage
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth
Excellent throughput for software compilation and Redis operations
Best suited for multi-threaded compute, web serving, and containerized microservices
